# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that downloads build artifacts from external URLs provided as parameters and deploys them to production servers. This allows us to deploy pre-built artifacts from different sources (S3, Artifactory, HTTP servers) without rebuilding.
# Category: cicd_security

import jenkins.model.*
def server = Jenkins.instance // get instance (Singleton)  
// Get the global node configuration object by calling NodeConfiguration.get() on our singleton master system resource in JENKINS_HOME environment variable or directly from local file /etc/jenkins-slave/.m2 if Maven is available and Jenkins's home directory has been set via 'JENKINS_URL'.
def nodeConfig = NodeConfiguration.get() // Returns configuration of the current instance, which defaults to a singleton resource in JENKINS_HOME (i.e., /var/lib/jenkins), but can be from local file system or any location where Jenkins is run on your machine using Maven and set environment variable $JENKINS_URL as well if it's not present inside build slave
def pipelineNode = nodeConfig.get(org.codehaus.mojo.versions.detectors.DetectionStrategy.STRICT) // Detected by version detector strategy - returns configuration for the current instance, strict detection (uses Jenkinsfile and Maven pom files to detect dependencies if not available).
def workspace = new Workspace("${env['WORKSPACE']}", 'empty', nodeConfig.get()) 
node('labeled') { // define a label or task/stage with "build in parallel" feature (allows us the tasks run at once)  
    stage ('Download build artifacts from external URLS provided as parameters and deploy to production servers' ){      
        for(url_param  in  env.params){          // loop through all parameterized values, each representing one url            
            echo "URL: ${env['WORKSPACE']}"          
            $ artifactName = ""             
         }    
    }  
}
